bodies.content: A little over a year ago, I decided it was time to delete Snapchat. Looking back on it, it was one of the best decision of my life. While the decision reduced my communication with more distant friends, it greatly reduced my phone screen time and made my value my close friendships. Basically quality over quantity, you know? Well, I think the time has come for another social media culling in my life.
Twitter

I've noticed a good majority of my phone screen time is spend on Twitter. Not even the app either, I go to the website in the browser. If this time was productive, I wouldn't think much of it, but that couldn't be further from the truth. Most things I see on Twitter are manga spoilers, horrible sports/F1 takes, and general bait posts to get interaction. While there are diamonds in the rough, I would say a majority of my timeline falls into one of those categories.

With all that in mind, I am going to deactivate my Twitter account. That may seem pretty drastic, but I want to complete remove myself from the platform and prevent the urge to slowly return to it. I do expect myself to get out of the loop in terms of memes and bleeding-edge news, but that's okay. I don't think I need to know what is happening everywhere in the world in real time.
This Blog

That brings us here, this blog! Twitter was the main platform I used for posting my thoughts and I still want some way to do that, but this will be different! Twitter was limited to a character limit, so a lot of my thoughts were limited by that. This blog is built by me so I control the limit and there isn't one! This allows me to really delve into thoughts that Twitter never allowed me to do. So I can go off on whatever topic I want! From serious stuff like personal health to goofy things like the history of tin cans. One last thing, I don't care if anyone ends up reading these, I think it's fun to create an archive of thoughts that I can go back and read as well.

While at work today, I was chatting with one of my friends and the topic of personal mindsets came up. She was mentioning how she needed a "change of mindset" and this really got me thinking, what is my mindset?

Within about a second, I knew my answer. When I was about 15 years old, I was making fun of my sister for getting a traffic ticket for rolling through a stop sign like all good little brothers should. Without skipping a beat, she just replied to my teasing with a smiling shrug while sarcastically saying "You can't win 'em all!". I'm not sure why this statement struck such a cord with me, but I remember this moment and those words so vividly. And since that day, whenever I come across some sort of hardship in my life, I always remember my sister saying it to me.
While simple, I think this phrase carries a couple of important reminders within it.
Keep Going

The most straightforward meaning to get out of this phrase, just keeping trying. It's a reminder that you will fail at some point in life. There will be bumps in the road. When you hit one of those bumps, it's important to keep your head up, endure the struggle, and learn for the future.
Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone

Another meaning to you can extract from this phrase is you're not alone in your failure. There is not a single person alive that is exempt from this part of the human experience. Even the most perfect people in the world will undergo some sort of hardship. So next time you start comparing yourself to people who seemingly go through life without any resistance, remind yourself it's not always like that for them. They can't win 'em all.
You Can Lose 'em All

Lastly, it is interesting to think about the complete opposite phrase. "You can lose 'em all". I think this phrase is also true. If you put in zero effort into your life, you will live a very unhappy life. Throwing in the towel is not a solution that bears any fruit.

So yeah, if you were wondering what my mindset is and how I stumble through life, this is it. I just mutter to myself, "You can't win 'em all" and lock the fuck back in. Thank you Miranda for setting such a great example <3

Since I've moved into my new apartment, I've been neglecting my hobby of bird photography. I could never work up the energy to get out there and take photos. If I had to give a reason, I think it's being in a new place and finding (free) spots to take photos is hard. At my parents' house, it was so easy with all the birds that were just in our backyard.

Last night at around 11pm, I decided I wanted to change that. I set my alarm for 7am and prepared my camera bag to go shooting. I've done this in the past, but when the time came, I always decided I was too tired and skipped. Today was different though, I sprung out of bed, got ready, and headed off to the lake.

I am so happy I did because I took some amazing photos today! I got shots of a Bald Eagle and a King Fisher, two birds I've been trying to find for so long! Along with the photos, it was also so relaxing hanging out by the lake for a couple hours, just basking in the morning sun and taking in the nature.

This trip really reminded me why I got into nature photography in the first place. It does an amazing job at breaking up the monotonous schedule of corporate life with the randomness and beauty of nature. It also provides with me so many avenues to learn new things like bird behavior, photography techniques, camera technology, and more!
So if you have been neglecting a hobby that has previous brought you enjoyment, get back out there, I'm sure you won't regret it! :D
P.S. There will be a bird photo section on this website soon so tuned!

A couple months ago, some friends and I started a vanilla Minecraft server and, the crazy part, we are still active on it! We have broken the two-week Minecraft server curse that so many people find themselves in! I believe we have found our long-term success on this server due all of us maintaining a healthy dose of Minecraft each week.

We have "Minecraft Mondays" where we all try and get on to do things together like boss fights, show off new builds, or just simply hangout. On any other day, it's up to the individual if they want to play on the server. Need to take some time away to do something else? Perfect! Want to play every night to finish a build? Also perfect!

The main point is nobody is forced into grinding to get things done in fear they will miss out on doing those things before the server dies. We set a clear goal from the beginning that we wanted a long-term server and I think that has helped everyone's mindset and fostered this healthy relationship.

One awesome part of this is the longevity of the server is it has allowed all of us to build bigger things than what would have been possible on a two-week long Minecraft binge. I normally am not much of a builder, but even I am dipping my toes into the water of it! So if you've been itching to play some Minecraft with friends, maybe try to promote this approach to extend the life of your server. It has made me very happy and I think it could do the same for you!

On Saturday, I decided to make a trip out to the Microcenter in Charlotte and, holy cow, that store is literally heaven. I am glad it is in Charlotte and not Raleigh or I would spend way too much money there. There are so many electronics that makes me want to start so many different projects. Their Raspberry Pi section alone was huge!

My reason for my visit was to build a new micro computer. Sure I could have ordered all the parts on Amazon and saved time, but what's the fun in that? It is way better to pick out the parts on the shelf myself. I also got to see Clark as a part of this trip which is worth it on it's own! <3
The Specs

This micro computer is centered around the Ryzen 7 8700G CPU. There are a couple reasons I chose to power my rig with this badboy. For one, it is a fast CPU with 16 threads running at 4.2 GHz, boosting up to 5.1 GHz. Being the "G" varient of the CPU, it also has an integrated GPU, the 780M, which has with 12 dedicated GPU cores running at 2900 MHz. All of this while only running at 65 W TDP, allowing me to use the Noctua L9 low profile with no other cooler solution. Since configuration uses system RAM as VRAM, I decided to get two sticks up 16 GB DDR5 RAM running at 6400 MHz CL32. Laslty, I got a simple Crucial 1 TB M.2 NVME drive for storage.
Pics
This computer came out tiny by utilizing the InWin Chopin Max case. It comes in at only 3.3 liters of space! Here it is compared to a can of Miller Lite.
But Why?

Some of you might be skeptical of this build with it lacking ultra-gaming prowess. And to be honest, you're skepticism is justified, I didn't need to build this computer. It is far less powerful than my current computer which has a 2070 Super, easily handling graphics in modern triple-A games.

However, I didn't build this computer to upgrade my gaming set up. I built it because I wanted to venture deep into the small form factor space and this is where I found myself. I wanted something really small and APUs peaked my interest!

Along with this, I also want gaming to take a more backseat role in my life, so this computer will lean into that mindset. I will still be able to play my favorite evergreen games like Risk of Rain 2 and Minecraft, however, it will add a little bit of a roadbump to play more graphically intensive games. But if I do decide I need to play a more graphically intensive game, I can pull out my old computer with it's massive GPU.
But yeah, it was a really fun build and it looks so cute on my desk! :D
Also, as you can see, I finally added photo integration in my blogs! Be ready for future blogs to include tons of fun photos!

Today, October 9th, 2024, officially marks eight years since I broke back. Eight years is such a weird length of time. Like it sounds like a long time, but it also feels like it was just yesterday. I wonder at what length of time it will feel like a long time ago. Maybe 17 years since that will mean half of my life was spent in a wheelchair. I'm not sure, check back in later and I'll let you know.

An even weirder part is almost everyone I talked to on a daily basis only knows me as wheelchair Will. I think non-disabled people could say something similar, like "most people only know post-high school me". But walking Will vs wheelchair Will feels different since it was such a sudden change that happened in a moment. Most other people change over a long period of time in small doses everyday.

This blog almost turned into a huge discussion on self-identity, but I think it would be better to dedicate an entire blog post for it. Then I have more time to think and formulate big big ideas that will make you say "Hmmm interesting...".

Anyways, yeah eight years of skipping leg day, pretty nuts. Really nothing else to say about it. I think I will follow in the footsteps of Microsoft and Apple and skip nine years and go straight to ten years next year.
One more thing before I end this blog, I got new glasses today! I don't do this very often so it's a big occasion! Check them out!
That's all for today. I've got a big plan in the works right now, so stay tuned.
byeeeeee :3

Last week, I was chatting with Nate in the office about random stuff as we usually do. One topic that came up in our conversation was programming and how it has changed from the schoolwork we had in college to the tasks we have at work. We both mentioned how we were fond of the programming problems in school that really needed some thinking like writing path finding algorithms. Since this conversation, these thoughts have lingered in my mind and I am going to do something about. So I have devised a small plan to get back to low-level programming.
The Book

During senior year of college, I started learning the programming language, Rust. For those unaware, Rust has been a big challenger to C when it comes to being the low-level programming king. Many have challenged C in the past, but Rust has been the most legit contender that I've ever seen. The main benefit it has over C is Rust is VERY memory safe. I could keep going on the benefits of Rust, but I think that is better saved for later on once I'm an expert.

To get into Rust, there is The Book. It's a free online book that walks you through all the syntax, quirks, and standard library of Rust. I've gotten up to chapter four in The Book previously and, my god, the documentation is amazing. I look forward to revisiting it. Also, people who write code in Rust call themselves "Rustaceans" and they has a little crab mascot named Ferris! Very cute!
Here is the URL for The Book in case you are interested, https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/
Warm Up

Going through The Book is only the beginning of this story. I plan on doing some mini-projects afterwards to really test my knowledge of Rust and low-level programming. This would probably include some sort of simple programming like sorting methods, dynamic programming questions, or any other type of algorithm questions. I just want to make sure I am familiar with project setups, file structures, and use data objects. I know Rust is pretty functional and I do mostly object-orientated programming at work, so it will be a little bit of a mindset flip. Bring me back to my Prolog and Racket days!
Big Boy Project

After I have honed my Rust abilities, it's time to really put them to the test. I think it would be fun to make my own game in Rust, which we need an engine for. I know what you're thinking, if I want to make a game, why not just dive into Godot or Unreal? Well where is the fun in that? The whole point of this is to get back into fun programming problems. Making games with already built engine feels so abstracted. I want to be in the math!

I'm not sure what the format of the game will be yet, 3D scares me, but who knows, I might be up for it when the time comes. I've been watching a lot of boomer-shooter development videos and I'm feeling inspired.
Vamanos!

All of this pretty high-level at the moment because I'm just getting started. I plan on writing blogs about updates with more details as time passes. If you want to join me in this journey, do it! The Book is free so why not give it a shot! Who knows, we could even make a game together and quit our corporate jobs!  ConcernedApe moment!

Here is my photo diary of the fair trip today! They are high resolution photos, so I'm sorry if they take a second to load. I was too lazy to compress them. Take it as a plus and try zooming to see all those sweet, sweet pixels.
That's all, folks!

About three weeks ago, I made a blog post about my plan to delve into Rust programming. An outline of my journey to get back into low-level programming like my college days. Well, I regret to report I've only made it through the first three chapters of the Rust book in 21 days. I would say I have no one to blame but myself for this, but I do have someone else to blame! Nate!

Around the same time I started the Rust book, Nate coerced me into replaying Hollow Knight along side him. Okay, that's not really true. In reality, there wasn't much coersion. Nate did ask me about replaying it and I agree pretty much instantly. I like Hollow Knight a lot and I thought replaying it sounded fun. Also my first (and only) playthrough of Hollow Knight, I only completed the bad ending, so it sounded nice to go back and complete the good ending.
While Nate isn't really to blame for me neglecting Rust for the Hollow Knight base game, I would say he is at blame for me and the post-game of Hollow Knight.

For those unaware, Hollow Knight has an area called 'Godhome' where you can go and rechallenge the bosses you defeated in the base game over and over again. Nate has nicknamed this area as the 'Hyperbolic Time Chamber' and he couldn't be closer to the truth. You can spend hours in Godhome just beating up on bosses that might have given you trouble in the story. You can try different upgrade combinations to see what works best or add to the challenge and fight an upgraded version of the boss.

Fighting the bosses individually isn't the real challenge of Godhome, however. The individual fights mearly serve as practice for the real purpose of Godhome, the Pantheons. The Pantheons are a series of five gaunlets, each consistenting of bosses from the story that you need to fight back-to-back. Mess up and die on the last boss of a Pantheon? Time to restart that Pantheon.

As you complete Pantheons and move up the ladder, they get progressively harder until you finally reach the fifth one and final one, the Pantheon of Hollownest. In this Pantheon, you are required to defeat all 43 bosses in the game in a row without dying. Patheon 5 is the pinacle of skill, where true Hollow Knight players, like Nate, go to flex their skills.

I really had no intention of entering Godhome at all after my playthrough. I had plenty of stuff on my plate like Rust programming and other games, but Nate had different plans for me. He gave me little nudges to get in there, saying things like, "You should try Pantheon 1, it's pretty easy!". After very little convincing, I gave into his wishes. Little did I know, these Pantheons would be like drugs to my Dark Soul[s].

Once I got a taste of the challenge, I couldn't stop. All I could think about was reaching the peak of Godhome. Just one more try. I just gotta move a little faster and I could beat this Pantheon. I just gotta get a couple more hits in and I could beat this Pantheon. I can beat all these Pantheons.

So that's my goal now. I will beat the Pantheon of Hollownest. As it stands right now, I have defeated Pantheon 1 and Pantheon 2. I am currently practicing for Pantheon 3, a big ramp up from the first two.
Also, I have decided that once I complete Pantheon 5, Nate and I will be getting Hollow Knight tattoos. Why? Don't ask me, I don't make the rules (I do.)
No cost too great.
No mind to think.
No will to break.
No voice to cry suffering.
Born of God and Void.
You shall seal the blinding light that plagues their dreams.
You are the Vessel.
You are the Hollow Knight.
I know, I know. It's been a while!

To tell you the truth, I've been itching to write a blog, but I've had nothing to write about. There have been a lot of little ideas floating around in my head, but nothing that could hold enough water to write an entire blog about.

This is what I was thinking until I remembered this is MY blog! There is no character maximum OR minimum! Ideas can be as big or as little as I want them to be! So buckle up, I have a lot of small blogs coming your way!
Movie Idea

When it comes to horror movies, I'm not normally a big fan. A lot of the ideas are just way too outlandish to actually scare me and keep me up at night. I don't think it's fair to criticize something without at least thinking how you could do it better. For me, good horror is grounded in reality. Something that could actually happen to you with no warning and mess you up. With that in mind, here is a movie idea!

The movie would begin with a normal dude in his mid-twenties, let's call him Alex. Alex is just living a typical life. He has a girlfriend, solid friends, a well-paying job, and the works. One night while out with some friends, he smokes some marijuana, nothing atypical for him, but something is not right afterwards. He feels like there is someone constantly whispering in his ear. After about a week, Alex decides to go to the doctor because nothing has changed.

The doctors learn about his marijuana habits and diagnose him with drug-induced chronic psychosis. They get him on an antipsychotic and tell him to stay away from drugs. However, Alex's issues were much larger than just a little prolonged psychosis. He is predisposed to schizophrenia and his recreational drug usage has moved up his timeline and his number is called.

The antipsychotic drugs help for a while, but eventually his mind starts to fall apart. Alex convinces himself that the doctors are lying and stops taking the drugs, making it his descent in madness even faster.

The rest of the meat and potatoes of the movie is his experiences with paranoid schizophrenia. Seeing his hallucinations and delusions grow and take over his mind in real time. As a filmmaker, this would be an opportunity to add some wild elements of eldritch horrors, like visuals you would see from the manga, Uzumaki. Look up some of the art if you don't know it.

While a majority of the movie will be shot through the eyes of Alex, there will be scenes shot from the perspective of his girlfriend, friends, and family. I haven't worked out the ending yet, maybe Alex kills himself, maybe he gets help, who knows.
FAQ
Will, this is fucked up... What the hell...?
Yeah, it is. Horror is meant to be fucked up and what's more terrifying than losing your mind?
Would people impacted by schizoaffective disorders appreciate this movie?
No, I'm not dumb, this concept is pretty insensitive and people will be pissed off.
Does any of this matter?

No, this movie will never be made due to it's problematic nature and honestly broad scope. But that's kind of the point. When you start thinking about things without silly limitations of what is possible to get done, you can come up with some fun concepts like this.
Well, that's all for today, folks. Stay tuned for more frequent blogs to come :)
I have found myself in quite the dilemma. Little note before reading, I wrote this all right now and didn't want to proof-read it. I'm sorry if there are any big grammar issues. Okay, let's run it.

My apartment lease ends on February 27th, 2025 and I need to tell my leasing office my intentions after this lease with a 60-day notice, aka December 27th, 2025. I was thinking about renewing my lease, but I also wanted to look around a little bit first since this is the time to make changes. I checked out an apartment in Carrboro that was really nice and would work with accessibility, but I didn't sign anything. While looking for other apartments, I decided to check out Zillow and see how the housing market is doing as well, this was a big mistake. I ended up finding a really really nice house, like I'm considering buying this house. So let's talk about whether this is a good or bad idea using SWOT!
What is SWOT?

SWOT is a goofy corpo tool used to analyze some sort of action or inaction you could take using the four categories that make up it's name: strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats. This is the mindset I had when thinking of items for each category.
Strengths: What does this house do well? What is unique about it that is beneficial?
Weaknesses: What could this house improve on? What is unique about it that is not beneficial?
Opportunities: What opportunities does this house open to me? What trends could you take advantage of? How can you turn your strengths into opportunities?
Threats: What threats does this house bring that could harm me? What are your peers doing? What threats do your weaknesses expose to you?
So let's SWOT this house!
Strengths
1. Two-Car Garage -- This is a very rare feature for houses in my price range. To be honest, I have to double my price range to find two-car garages anywhere within 30 minutes of the office.

2. Unrenovated Interior -- One thing that really grinds my gears is the soulless renovations that get done on starter homes. The plain white cabinets, grey vinyl floor, every wall white, and everything else super shit. This house has none of that.

3. Great Maintenance -- This house was clearly owned by an elderly man who took great pride in his property. Every little improvement to home was done with great intention. Things like bug screens in the crawl space vents and amazing record keeping for maintenance on A/C and furnace.

4. Brick House -- If you know my taste, you know I am a lover of brick. The front of this home is all natural brick, something you never find a one a new build due to the expensive nature of brick. The brick has also been upkept very well with the mortar in great condition. It was also built well, containing the proper brick "weep" hole for allowing moisture to escape from behind the brick wall.

5. Large Bathroom -- Another thing that is very hard to find in houses in my price range is large bathrooms. Due to my wheelchair, I just need more space to be able to get on the toilet and get in the shower.
6. Gated Community -- Safety is always something to keep in mind when picking a place to live. This house is in a gated golf course community so there is nothing but peace of mind here.

7. New Big Ticket Items -- There are a lot of really expensive things in a house that last a long time but are expensive to replace. This is your air conditioner, furnace and hot water pump. All of these things on this home have been replaced in 2019 and have a lot of life left in them.
Weakness
1. Location -- This house is located in Sanford, same as my parents' house. This is not very close to pretty much any fun activity.

2. Ramp -- To access this house at all right now, I would need to build a ramp. This isn't that big of a deal, this is something I excepted to have to do when buying a home. Still sucks since it would prevent me from moving in right away.

3. Small Renovation Needed -- Remember how I mentioned how it has not been renovated? Yeah, I meant it. The paint and the carpet is starting to show it's age and those would need to be taken care. This doesn't have to be done right away, but it is easier when the house is empty.

4. Original Roof -- While pretty much every other big item for the house has been renewed in the past five years, the original roof from 2006 is still on the house. It isn't horrible, just a there are a couple shingles starting to peel up. Talking to my dad, he said he gives it another five to eight years of life before a replacement is a must.
5. Home Owner's Association -- Being in a gated community, of course it has an HOA. It is $80 a month which is annoying, but not the end of the world.
Opportunities

1. Explore Hobbies -- Having a house would allow me to explore hobbies that I couldn't do with an apartments. For example, I really want to get a 3D printer but I just have no space for it. I would also love to get into small woodworking projects.

2. More Permanent Living Space -- Owning a house is really nice because I do not need to worry about renewing a lease every 15 months. I can also make improvements to the space as I see fit since it's mine.

3. Bigger Living Space -- With more rooms, I can dedicate rooms to specific tasks. The biggest one being a separate office and bedroom. I could even have a separate work office and gaming set up room, or go for a more convential setup with a guest room. There are lots of different layouts I could explore.

4. Shifted Financial -- Right now my main financial goal is building up a bigger and bigger down-payment for a house. If I buy a home, I can shift my goals to saving for things to improve my house which is a lot more fun!

5. Low Maintenance Yard -- The yard doesn't have grass, it's all pretty much covered in pine straw, the North Carolina classic. For someone like my dad, this would kill him. For me, this is great because it is almost no maintenance.
6. Generator -- This house has a generator piped into the natural gas system. So if there was ever an issue with power, I could switch the generator for needs like refrigerator.
Threats

1. Long Commute -- The main threat to this house is the location and frequency I will have to drive into the office. The best-case scenario commute time would be 45 minutes. This is a very similar commute that I did when I lived with my parents, but traffic is greater now that the I-540 loop toward Fuquay has been opened.

2. Far Away Friends -- Being about an hour away from all of my friends is tough because it pretty much eliminates the potential for last minute plans. These don't happen too often, but it still does happen. It also put strain on other hang out plans since I need to plan my drive into the hang out.
3. More Permanent Living Space -- Owning a house is really scary because I am locked in this house for at least four-ish years.
4. Wear on Car -- Commuting to work alone would result in 13,000 miles on my car per year. This would be about three oil changes per year and a new set of tires every three years.
5. Gas -- More on driving, I will need to buy a lot more gas to allow me to drive a lot.

6. Dating Pool -- Living in Sanford creates an interesting situation for dating. Let's say I meet a girl while hanging out in Durham or Raleigh, how would she feel hearing that I own a house in Sanford? Would the right girl for me care? Hard to say but another thing to keep in mind as a potential threat.
Final Thoughts
It's very eye-opening writing this all out and I have a lot of things to think about. As you can tell, this would be a no-brainer if it wasn't for the commute.

A big thought in my mind is I spend a lot of time at home, like a majority of my time is spent at home. With that being the case, doesn't it make sense to prioritize the space at home than the drive away from my home?

I would love to hear your thoughts based on everything I've discussed here. Here is a picture of the house as well as the link to realtor dot com. Feel free to check it out. The realtor dot com will show it's off-market, but that's not true.
https://www.realtor.com/realestateandhomes-detail/1709-Chateau-Cir_Sanford_NC_27332_M53885-97759
This is a follow-up to my last blog where I did an in-depth analysis of purchasing a house. Well, after a couple grueling days, I have made my decision on what is coming next.
I will not be buying the house.
The main deciding piece of not buying it is timing. I think this decision could have gone much different if I was five years older.
Liquid

In five years, I will have a lot more liquid due to having more time to save. Right now, pretty much all of my liquid would go into the down payment, closing costs, and building a ramp for the house. This would leave no money to begin the renovation process, which this house needed. Sure it would be "liveable" right away, but I want it to be comfortable as soon as possible which wouldn't have been soon enough with my current finances. Hell, I wouldn't really have a true emergency fund which is a bad spot for a new home owner!
Location

I don't think the location was the end of the world. I think the commute would be a fine trade-off for all the benefits the house was providing. However, me being 25 years-old, I think I want to push off moving to burbs for a little bit. I think this is the time where I should get an apartment where things are walkable and I can do fun things very often. Then when I am older, I can move to the burbs and spend my evenings wrenching away in a garage.
Lessons Learned

Spending this past week thinking about buying this house has really opened my eyes. There are a couple big milestones in life that everyone wants to achieve like getting that career job, finding a spouse, buying a house, etc. We all want to achieve these things so we are all gunning towards them, however, I think it's a good idea to slow down sometimes. Things will happen when the time is right, no reason to force things. So yeah, for now, I am going to continue renting until the right time comes :)
Thank you to everyone who gave me their input, I valued it greatly!
Happy New Year to all my blog readers! This blog is kind of late coming out January 7th, but better late than never!
New Year, Same Sponge

New Year's resolutions are something kind of goofy to me. I don't think you should ever wait until a new year to make a healthy change in your life. If an idea comes to you in July or November, it is so dumb to say "Well I'll start it in the new year". No! Start right away! Use that momentum to hit the ground running!

However, when the new year comes around, I think it is a great time to be self-reflective when you might not have had time to be earlier in the year. Look at yourself in the mirror and ask yourself how you can be a better you. In a perfect world, this is something we would do everyday, but things get busy and we forget to check up on the person we should be checking up on the most.
So as I look in the mirror, there are two main things I would like to change. I work too much and I have too much empty time. So I asked myself, what can I do to change these?
Work Less

Working too much manifests in two ways in my life. First, I find myself working outside of my predefined hours like doing things past 5pm as well as sometimes on the weekends. Sometimes this is unavoidable, but it should be VERY limited and it hasn't been. Second, I do too much work during work hours. Sounds crazy, but I need to spend more time slacking off. It's that simple. I don't need to respond to coworkers on teams right away, I don't need to finish stories immediately, and I don't need to spend 8 hours in the office. So for those of you who work with me, help me out with this, I'm sure it won't be too hard, some of you already do this so thank you :)
Reduce Empty Time

Whenever I have no plans and I'm feeling kind of lazy, I will find myself just sitting in front of my computer doing nothing but scrolling YouTube or Instagram. This time is wasted in my opinion. Excluding some specific YouTube channels, this is nothing but consuming brain rot media. I want to be more present in my own free time and use it to become a better, more well-rounded person. Some things I will be doing to accomplish this is reading, programming, photo editing, or blogs! All of these things can still be done from the comfort of my own apartment but are way more productive!

So yeah! That's the summary of my new year reflection session. I need to reflect on my current state more often, but like I said earlier, things get busy and it gets pushed to the back burner. I guess that's a secret third thing to work on ;)
And so I'm building a bridge
Between what is and all that will be
That will never be done
I lay a stone for each step
Try not to think of what's ahead or
If I'll always need one
- Be Fine, Roland Faunte

title: Devil went down to the Durham Orchestra

bodies.content: In the middle of December, I went to a Durham orchestra concert to cheer on one of my friends from college. I've been going to a majority of her shows since freshman year and they are highlights of my year. This show was centered around the theme of Earth and taking care of our planet. The pieces were all standard orchestra music, except for one. The piece that was the exception was called "The Automatic Earth" composed by Steven Bryant. It consists of a typical orchestra but an added twist of digital sounds played along side.
If your curious to give it a listen before reading, here is a video of the University of Indianapolis performing this piece, https://youtu.be/WpP7Qo-3gZE?si=Fl-RGvoboD3ytXnF .
My Impressions on The Automatic Earth

First of all, I think the auditorium I was in didn't have the best sound system installed because all of the electronic sounds were clipping when they would reach a certain volume. The sound clipping would remove me from the music almost instantly which was disappointing. Listening to the YouTube recording above honestly sounded a lot more clean. An example of this is at 21:15 in the video where pretty much a doomsday siren is playing. This thing sounded awful in the auditorium I was in. So I think my thoughts overall are pretty skewed.

Moving past that though, listening this in person was pretty interesting. It was unusually hearing a full orchestra "fight" for space in my ears with electronic sounds. I say "fight" because the sounds did not mesh together AT ALL. One was always trying to overcome the other. This goes past the clipping I mentioned earlier too. Even the quiet moments, I was always separating the two voices in my head. However, watching the video, it's the opposite, I think they mesh perfectly together fine. I think this might have to do with the raw, uncompressed sound you get by listening to string instruments in person. This lends them to achieving a completely different range that the digital sounds can't compete with. But this "fight" gave me an idea!
My Idea

It gave me an idea for a potential orchestral piece. First, let's go over the arrangement. There would a full orchestra accompanied by two violin soloists. One violinist would have just a traditional, wooden violin, but the other would have an electric violin and a TON of pedals.

The first movement would begin with the two soloists going back and forth like a guitar battle. Think The Devil Went Down to Georgia but like more classical. In this first movement rest of the orchestra would play a very small role and the electric violinist is not using any pedals. As the first movement comes to a close, it is clear the traditional is winning the battle due to it's cleaner sound.

To begin the second movement, the electric violinist decides it is time to up the ante (Balatro reference!) and starts to bring in the pedals, adding unique sounds effects like reverb. The traditional violinist tries to pull out some different techniques like plucking, but just can't keep up with the variety of the pedals.

Just when all hope is lost for the traditional violinist, the third movement begins with the rest of the orchestra coming and playing a huge role with traditional violinist. The power of the entire orchestra playing off the soloist is extreme and the electric violist is overwhelmed and fails to stand their ground. With no options, the electric violinist accepts defeat in their battle.

The fourth and final movement is not a victory lap, but a birth of a new friendship. The traditional violist invites the electronic violinist to accompany them with the orchestra. The dual now turn into a duet of traditional and modern style. And they played fire on the mountain, run, boys run! (haha iykyk).
My Thoughts

This might sound absolutely insane for someone who doesn't listen to orchestral music. Like how could you tell a story like this with no words? I'm not sure how composers do it, but I have heard some super vivid storytelling in orchestral pieces. Thinking about my idea, I can hear what I think it should be, but I have no idea how to put it to paper. I hope someone more talented than me can make it happen one day.

This might also sound insane to someone who loves and knows orchestral music. I don't have too much knowledge into historical context of what is ~required~ for writing stuff like this, but I don't care, this is how innovation happens. I'll bet a fiddle of gold against your soul because I think I'm better than you!

P.S. This orchestra concert also featured the song "Earth" composed by Penka Kouneva and it was my favorite piece of the entire show, highly recommend giving it a listen. The violin solo in the beginning was so intense in person, I wish everyone I know could have experienced it. The rest of the piece is very nice too, very big Planet Earth vibes. I thought David Attenborough was going to start talking about wildlife when it started winding down lol.
P.P.S You like the new year categories for the blogs? I think it will be easier to have the old blogs hidden since I want to write a lot more :)
With the first month of the year wrapping up, I wanted to do a quick check-in with everyone about my goals that I set for myself for the new year!
Productive Empty Time

When it came to reducing empty time and being more productive, I'm on a great path right now. Many moments this month I found myself doom-scrolling and I would take a step back and ask myself, "What could I be doing right now that is not this?" I would then pivot to something like reading, playing video games, cleaning, researching apartments (hehe), or whatever else!

I can't say I'm perfect and corrected this every time, but I would say the rates were pretty dang good! This might come as a surprise, but this was really challenging to do when the elevator was broken and I was stuck in my apartment. There was something about having no schedule of coming and going that just melted my brain for that week. Like I would tell myself, "Now would be a good time to read" and I would end up napping instead. There's probably some psychological explanation for this, but I couldn't tell ya!

Getting into that, one of the things I've been doing to fill my time is reading! Just to peak into the numbers. Last year I read only one book that was 300 pages long. This year, I am finishing up my second book with about 550 total pages read! Hell yeah!

Compared to other people, these numbers might sound pathetic, but I don't care about them. I am making good progress compared to what I was doing last year, and as we all know, the only person you should ever compare yourself to is who you were yesterday.

In case you haven't noticed, I also set up a book tracker on my website so you can check in on what I'm currently reading, what I plan on reading, and what I've finished! If you see something you wanna chat about or want to recommend anything, let me know!
Work Less

So... This one is tough. At the time of writing this, I've worked the two previous Saturdays and I work tomorrow (Saturday). I have a feeling I will be doing another install next Saturday. This ridiculous schedule is due to the security audit that I am currently leading the remediation of as well as being on-call for my team. Pretty much the perfect storm for working a lot.

While Saturdays have been avoidable recently, I have been logging off my computer by 5:30pm though! Great improvement compared to before where I could find myself doing work well past 6:00pm! Baby steps, right?

I think the main crux of this matter is my team. I really need to find a new team in order to lessen my workload. I have been checking Workday at least twice a day, but no teams on there are tickling my fancy. I don't want to go to a do-nothing frontend team. I want something with some weight. Sure, this might introduce me to the same problem I'm trying to leave behind, but I can set up much better boundaries from the get-go. I think I am going to start reaching out to other teams I've worked with and see if they have any potential spots.
Recap

So yeah, that was fun! Things aren't going perfectly, but when does that ever happen? I don't plan on doing an update every month, but I think the end of January is a real make-or-break time when it comes to new year goals, so I figured I would hold myself accountable with this update.

So now I ask you, how are you doing on your goals? I hope you are making great progress and creating positive changes in your life. If you're slacking on executing them, take this as a sign to lock in! Don't keep your better self waiting!
Hey, Will, why don't you cut the shit
And tell me who you're fighting for?
If you're not taking care of yourself
Then what are you here for?
- Kimochi Warui, Car Seat Headrest

Being human, we all have the urge to create things. This could be literally anything from a dumb website to a beautiful painting or anything in between! However, in this pursuit of creation, we have all found ourselves in the situation where we just couldn't get the pen to paper and we end up with nothing. There are countless reasons why I've fallen victim to phenomenon, but whatever the reason, it always feels shitty to bail on creativity.
However, over the years, I feel like I've gotten better about this and there are three mains things that helped me in doing this.
Lower Your Expectations

I will get the perfect of idea for something like a video game to make or a photo to take, but I just never get the right first step and it screws it all up. There are always little things off in the beginning and these minor imperfections derail my motivation completely and I give up on the idea entirely.

While creating, I believe it is important to remind yourself it's okay to make mediocre things! You're not doing it as professional and it doesn't need to perfect. Some of my favorite photos I've ever take were on whim inside of my apartment. Not some fancy planned shoot on location! So if you get an inkling to create, just send it and don't worry about the end product until you're done. If it turns out shit, who cares? Better to make shit than make nothing at all.
Make Creating a Habit

Too many times when I have an idea for something to create, I schedule a dedicated time slot to do it. Once the time finally arrives, I will decide I'm "not feeling it" and then the idea is never brought into reality.

There is no reason why we need to wait for huge blocks of time to create. There are so many little moments in the day where you can toss in tiny bits of creativity. The more we do it, the more natural it will become and it will be less burdensome to do big tasks when they come.

Two years ago, I wanted to get more comfortable carrying a camera with me and taking photos no matter the environment around me. Practice makes perfect, so I started capturing a "photo of the day". For three and a half months, I had to pick at least one photo from my Ricoh GRIIIx to be the PotD! This forced me to find photos in a bunch of nothingburger days. Like the previous thing I mentioned, I ended up taking some of my favorite photos during this period.
For this challenge, I also took all the photos in black and white. It made me think differently while taking photos than how I normally did with bird photography.
Fuck the Algorithms

I see you. I'm guilty of it too. Get off Instagram reels, Tiktok, or whatever else is wasting your time with mindless scrolling. Think those Saturdays where you just laid in bed for hours just scrolling. Think of how much cool stuff you could have created in that time. We all need time to relax, but we all indulge more than we should.
Play Ball!

With all these little tips to get started, we could create some banger shit. These tips also work hand-in-hand, so implementing one will lead to implementing them all! Creating is so stimulating and your brain will love you for it! You'll have so much serotonin, you won't know what to do with it! So yeah, what are you still doing reading this? Get your ass to work!

title: Been a Long Time Since I Rocked and Rolled

bodies.content: Over the weekend, I went on a trip to Asheville to see a Led Zeppelin cover band with my sister and my dad. The cover band we saw was "Zoso". While other cover bands will base their sets off the studio production side of the Led Zeppelin, Zoso are known to be the most true cover band to the Led Zeppelin concert experience. Let me tell ya, they nail that. I've seen the documentary "The Song Remains the Same" which is just a bunch of old concert film of Led Zeppelin and Zoso must have studied the shit out of it. The performance was identical from obvious things like the outfits to little things like the stage presence of each member. I talked to a boomer at the show who had seen both Zoso and Led Zeppelin in the past and he said "These guys are the real deal! You'll love this!" and he was correct!
That Damn Whale

I have loved Led Zeppelin all my life, going as far as saying they are my all-time favorite band. That being said, I never understood the hype around the famous drum solo on Moby Dick. I always thought it was cool, but not "to die for" like everyone claimed. However!!!! Now that I've heard the solo performed live, I totally understand these claims. It lasted about 15 minutes and had my attention the entire time. The drummer was just going crazy, but yet still so organized! I swear his hands were moving at one million miles per hour! Just like the real John Bonham, the drum ditched the sticks after a while and started playing with his hands! It was simply amazing. I can't even find the right words how epic it was. If you have any interest in Led Zeppelin AT ALL, I would highly recommend seeing this show for the Moby Dick solo alone.
Anyway, I don't need to Ramble On, enjoy some pics I got at the show! If you want to see Zoso, they are coming to Raleigh May 2nd and I would be down to go again :)

It is currently 7:33pm and I am sitting on my balcony nursing a cold Miller High Life. I am listening to the bird activity wind down with the setting sun, however, it might be time to play some Beatles in a bit as the chirping is fading faster and faster.

Sitting on my balcony like this always reminds me cliche quotes like "take in the little moments" or "don't forget to smell the roses".  Then those quotes then make me think of the opposite cliche quotes like "details form the big picture" or "keep your eyes on the prize".
So what is it? Does earning a prize require abstaining from taking part in the little moments? Does stopping to smells the roses blur the details of the bigger picture?

Sometimes my brain leads me to believe these ideas is true. Like why am I sitting on my balcony drinking a beer when I could be doing something infinitely more productive? How is this doing anything for me? Well, sitting out here drinking doesn't make sense and it is doing nothing for me. That is all true, but it is also perfectly fine.

I find myself sometimes getting swept up in my bigger picture goals and it sends me spiraling. I will think to myself, "what can I be doing in this moment to be moving forward in life?". This is such silly thinking because you know what bigger pictures need besides details? Time. Rome wasn't built in a day and neither is your life.

Given that things take time to complete, like years or even decades, unwinding by drinking a beer on a Saturday night is not only okay, but recommended! Stepping away and taking your eyes off of the prize can allow you to return later with a fresh perspective and achieve more than ever could before.
So go ahead and drink that beer, binge that TV show, or whatever else you do. It will be okay!

Some of you are probably thinking, "Duh? Was a blog really needed for this?". I figured if I've felt this way, I'm sure one of you probably has too. If you're looking some more important advice, wear sunscreen or something idk.

I don't know about any of you guys, but I have a lot of data, like 160 GB of documents, photos, videos, and whatever misc file types I have accquired over the years and tossed in my Google Drive! I really couldn't tell you when it got really high, but it did! Even before I started photography, I had like 100 GB of data!

Even though I have all this data, I wouldn't call myself a data hoarder. Every once in a while I comb through all of my data and remove unnecessary things like outdated paperwork and memes. This process may sound tedious, but it is actually really fun because I get to relive a lot of memories by looking at my old data, especially photos.

I was doing this recently and I stumbled across my old "Ricoh Photo of the Day" series I did a while back. For those unaware of this series, I was taking one photo a day with my Ricoh GRIIIx camera with a nice black and white filter. The photo was usually the highlight activity for the day like hanging out with friends, going out to a place, or whatever else might be happening. However, there were days where I didn't really do anything. I would use those days as a photography exercise to find a nice photo out of the mundane. So not only did this series create good memories, it also made me a better, more confident photographer.

With about two years having passed since I had ended my RPotD, I think it's time for a revival! That's right! Catch me carrying around my Ricoh again and be sure to smile for the camera :D Also, I am going to continue to take all these photos in black and white. I really like how the photos turned out and it makes the photos look so much better when lighting isn't favorable. Maybe that will change later, but I like the aesthetic still, so I am sticking with it.

I already started this a couple days ago, so enjoy the first photo of the RPotD revival :) Oh, I will not be lowering the quality of these photos. They WILL take a while to render on the webpage, it's worth it for the pixels

P.S. If you also have a lot of data and want to manage it in Google Drive, I HIGHLY recommend a product called "Insync". It makes it so easy to seamlessly work with cloud files on your local your computer and have your changes automatically backed up. If you wanna hear more about, let me know and I can show you my set up with it, or you can check out their website, https://www.insynchq.com/. The only downside is there is one-time fee of $40, but I can tell you it's SO worth it! I will say for my security-pilled fellas out there, rclone is better solution for ensuring your data is 100% by leverage private keys and having an open source codebase, but it's a bitch to use and you can't tell me otherwise.

On November 30th, 1849, 30,000 people gathered to watch the double hanging of Fredrick and Marie Manning in London. Among those spectators was the famously known Charles Dickens. Following the event, he wrote a letter to The Times about his thoughts. A small excerpt as follows,

I believe that a sight so inconceivably awful as the wickedness and levity of the immense crowd collected at that execution this morning could be imagined by no man, and could be presented in no heathen land under the sun. The horrors of the gibbet and of the crime which brought the wretched murderers to it faded in my mind before the atrocious bearing, looks, and language of the assembled spectators.
Charles Dickens

He goes on to insult the crowd more about their behavior and their audacity to show up to such a heinous event. Very crazy to go to a hanging, watch the entire thing, and look around and think "Wow, everyone here is barbaric... except for me." This blog is not that and I want that to be extremely clear. I am part of the problem and I do not claim to be holy than thou. So what is this problem? Hating.

I feel like whenever we express interest in quite literally anything, it will be met with some sort of resistance. It is so common to say something as simple as "Oh this weekend I had fun doing [anything]" and someone will reply with disgust, confusion, or contempt for no reason at all. It's weird... like why is this the normal thing to do after we hear someone's thoughts on something? Well I have a couple thoughts about it.
Our Language

I think one of the pitfalls that leads us to be haters all the time is the language we use without thinking about it unintentionally. When we talk about how we feel about something, it is so easy to use objective phrases like "This [thing] is good/bad." However, I think MOST of us really mean to say something more subjective like "I do/don't like this [thing]."

By stating our opinions as objective truths, it is basically opening the door to criticism because silence is agreeing and we do not like to agree to things if we don't agree! It is also so easy to leave off the reasoning on why we believe something, acting as if our opinion is some universal truth that is known.

If someone says to me, "Jordan is better than Lebron", I feel the urge to tell them it's a bad take. However, if they say, "Jordan's two championships over Lebron makes me think he is a better player", I don't feel the need to argue even though I disagree. By stating thoughts as thoughts instead of facts, it's easier to let things go.
Wait... It's All Art?

Going along with the subjective versus objective, I think we forget how much around us is art. Things like video games, music, movies, books, photography, television, and, hell, even certain playstyles of athletes could be argued as an artform. When it comes to art, there will always be some critic out there that just missed the point or wasn't part of the target audience. And that is okay! Art made for everyone is art made for no one! So no matter what you like, you are going to meet someone who disagrees with you eventually.
Devil on Your Shoulder

I've done a lot of talking about stopping the hate and enjoying your interests freely, but like all things, moderation is key. Opening up dialogues and hearing others' opinions on your interests can be fun! It can open you up to different perspectives and allow you to enjoy something even more. And not everything is perfect! Understanding where your interests fall short is important! Ignorance can be very annoying.

For example, I love the movie Tenet and I understand Tenet is a very polarizing movie. It is not friendly to first-time viewers due to its confusing plot and its sound design is very unconventional in how it is blasting music all the time. While these are all valid downfalls of which I accept, they also make me love Tenet that much more. I love sinking my teeth into the complex time theory Christopher Nolan cooked up in this movie. I also love hearing the intense score by Ludwig Goransson while just watching the actions of the actors to really understand what is going on. (The dialogue is lying to you! Stop reading the subtitles and watch!)
Even still, all of what I just said about Tenet, none of it is the objective truth. It's just how I feel about it.
But Like Why though?

Gotta ask ourselves, are we actually doing this for some other reason? Subconsciously are we roasting others because we are unhappy with ourselves and we hate seeing others be happy? And we want company in our unhappiness so we are willing to tear others down to our level? Or are we so obsessed with being correct all the time that we need to take every opportunity we can to disagree and win an argument? Was this whole blog just a ploy for me to talk about Lebron James and Tenet? More likely than you think!
Where to Go From Here?

Going forward, when someone likes something harmless and they tell us about it, there is no reason to tear down their beliefs if we don't jive with it. No reason to yuck their yams. And if you find someone yucking your yams, just call them out. Ask them why they don't want to see you happy. Maybe it will awaken something in them... or it won't, but worth a shot.
Sorry JENNIE, the new mantra is "I am not going to ship your ship because it doesn't float my boat."

P.S. I write a lot of blogs that die on the cutting-room floor. This blog was actually one of them that I wrote a mock draft of like a month ago. However, today I felt especially bad when it came to hating while talking about video games at lunch and it inspired me to pick this blog back up. Create some sort of paper trail on this effort idk.

P.P.S This is one of my favorite parts of writing these blogs. No way in hell someone would have listened to me say all this shit out loud, but it's nice to throw it all out in the open. Highly recommend others to begin their blogging journey :3

title: Thunder Only Happens When It's Raining

bodies.content: Do you guys ever think about your dreams? Not like your life aspirations, but the almost-random adventures your mind goes on every night. Do you think they have meaning?

For me, I typically do not remember my dreams very often. I know they are happening because I'm not braindead, but you'd think the memories of my dreams are vampires the way they are avoiding the sunrise. Not all of them, however, and I think there is a common thread of the ones that do survive the night.

The dreams that I do remember are all very realistic. It's always something along the lines of chatting with someone I know in a very recognizable place about well-known topics. I never remember those crazy dreams I hear others talking about like performing espionage inside of the Kremlin. But my dreams aren't completely normal, the weird bit is one of the aspects (the people, the topics, or the place) of the dream is always long in the tooth, like an ex-girlfriend from five years ago in my current apartment talking about my cat. Like how'd you get there and why do you know about any of this????
I've done some thinking about this and I have come up with a silly reasoning on why my dreams are like this and why I remember specifically these ones.

During REM, the brain goes crazy and neurons are firing everywhere. Why the brain is doing this is something that still confuses neuroscientists, but brain scans show that it is happening so yeah. For my theory, I think there is no rhyme or reason for what neural pathways are being taken by the brain during REM sleep, it is by purely chance. This means whether a path has been taken recently while awake doesn't matter. Every path has an equal chance.

Even though I do not think recency impacts if a path gets taken, I do think recency impacts the ability to remember taking a path during REM. This is because the neural pathway is "cached" in the consciousness, making it really easy for your consciousness to make sense of the pathway without having to access long-term memory before writing to short-term memory. On the flipside, if a neural pathway has not been used recently, it is not "cached" and requires the consciousness to make an expensive long-term memory read to make sense of the pathway before writing the meaning to short-term memory.

Every REM cycle, the brain can only handle reading so much from long-term for the dream. If the brain tries to read too much from long-term memory, it falls behind writing the wild dream to short-term causing holes, and the dream ends up not getting remembered at all. So in order to remember a dream, the brain has to leverage a certain amount of "cached" neural pathways to avoid falling behind writing to short-term memory. This results in only remembering dreams with a lot of recent memories sprinkled with some old memories.
So with all that in mind, do I think my dreams have any meaning to them? Nah, I think things are showing up in my dream purely by chance. Nothing more than a dice roll by my brain.

Do I actually believe all this as the reason for why I remember certain dreams and not others? No, not really, but it's a fun idea, right? I think it's fun to try and make sense of the unknown by forcing it through a lens of things I do know. Highly recommend giving it a try!
Brought to you by Spotify's Alice in Chains radio and Tahitian Treat!
(Large image files incoming!)
Here are my photos from the weekend at VIR :D

One of my favorite things to do with my time is getting deep into niche hobbies. I love building weird skills and finding out how deep some of these rabbit holes go. A favorite type of hobby I find myself peeking into often are precision sports. These are sports that are heavily focused on accuracy and precision like golf, target shooting, bowling, washers, corn hole, and many more! Today I am beginning a journey into one of these, pool!

I had a room in my house that was just screaming for a pool table and who I am to deny this very reasonable request. So about two weeks ago while I was buying furniture on Glenwood Ave, I drove past a store that had "Billards" in the name and I knew I had to stop in. After talking to a very cool salesman, I ended up purchasing a used pool table in great condition and it was delivered today! (ignore the unfinished room, it's in-progress of remodeling)
Why Buy a Pool Table?

One of my main issues with my bowling hobby was my accessibility to bowling lanes. It was a coin toss on whether I would get to play or not when I would show up to the bowling alley due to lanes being constantly filled. On top of that, when I would get a lane, it would be next to annoying people. I already know I like playing pool, so I felt comfortable taking the plunge and just purchasing a table to skip this whole annoying phase of traveling places to play.

A downside to playing at home is I will miss out on the community factor. I met some cool guys while hanging out at the bowling alley who gave me some insight on how to improve my game. While I'll be lacking that for my at-home practice, I can always make my way down to a bar with a pool table and try and hustle some beer money out of some old dudes or even learn a thing or two from them.

I think having my own pool table will be really useful to sucking any friends into the sport as well! It's much easier to convince people to play with me when all they have to do is come over to my place and they get access to free beer in the process. If you are reading this and want to play, let me know! :D
Cue Up

I've never been a consistent pool player, only playing maybe a handful of times every year while at the bar, so there's some learning to be done. That's a nice way of saying I am not good at pool right now so do not expect me to run the table. I'm trying to think of a simple way to track my progress like I did with bowling. Maybe something like the number of shots to finish a game? Or maybe some type of practice regiment that produces some sort of numerical value. Either way, keep an eye out on my website for the pool tracking page!

title: New Year Self Reflection 2: Electric Boogaloo

bodies.content: Yeah yeah, this blog is almost a week late. I wanted to make sure I was 100% on everything said in this blog, and I always say it's better to be late and confident than on time and self-doubting.

Reflecting on this year, it was honestly pretty freaking great. Going over the highlights quickly, I got to hang out with my friends a ton, Millicent became my roommate, I bought a house, Car Seat Headrest dropped a banger album, I got promoted at work, and so many other victories! 2025, you get a gold star in my book and you will be remembered fondly! All that stuff is amazing, but enough of the boasting, how well did I fare on personal growth with my goals?
2025 Goal Review
To refresh everyone's mind of last year's new year blog, my main goals were to work less and reduce my empty time.

Although from the outside it may seem I failed at working less based on my busy work schedule, I think I actually did a good job on following this goal. I did not work outside of business hours that wasn't absolutely needed. And when I did need to work outside of business hours, I made sure to get that time back by chilling during normal hours.

When it comes to reducing my empty time, I think I did an even better job on this goal! I got my screentime much lower by uninstalling social media off of my phone and forcing me to focus on things with more substance instead. I will admit for the month of December, I did spend a lot of time on Instagram since I had so much more chill time caused by slow days at work and vibing during the holidays. Now I am back on my normal schedule and I'm locking back in!

Along with those two goals mentioned, about a third of the way through the year I added another goal to save more money. I knew I didn't want to get another apartment after my Carrboro one, so I wanted to be in the financial position to buy a home by the time my lease was up. Considering I am typing this blog in my house, I would say this was very successful! This goal was very easy to remember because I made my old work password "SaveMoney!". That little daily reminder was so helpful to have for something easy to slip up on like frivolous spending.
2026 Goals

2026 has some big shoes to fill, but I have my sights set on two main goals this year to make it great! While last year goals were very self-improvement focused, we are going to have a little more fun this year, starting with hobby-maxing!

As you all know, I love trying out new hobbies, like breadth first search type deal. This year I want to focus on a couple hobbies and gain some true expertise in them, like depth first search type deal. After some thinking, I've decided I would focus on sim racing and billiards. Both of these things I can do from the comfort of my own home leaving room for zero excuses for lacking practice! This goal already started in December with me prepping my facilities for it, but the plan was for it to really ramp up in 2026.

A second goal of mine is to rebuild my financial standing. I have just spent an obscene amount of money and holy hell my bank account hurts to look at. I would like to replace it all so I'm not scared to open my brokerage account anymore. This goes along nicely with my hobby-maxing since both of my selected hobbies are bought and paid-for allowing me to follow a strict budget while still following my other goal!
But that's all! I'm sure you will all hear many check-ins about these goals throughout the year in both blog and verbal communication with me. Let's all ball out this year, folks!
I'm feeling rough, I'm feeling raw, I'm in the prime of my life.
Let's make some music, make some money, find some models for wives.
I'll move to Paris, shoot some heroin, and fuck with the stars.
Time to Pretend, MGMT

Working a desk job, I've found it is very easy to spend my entire day sitting in front of a screen. Wake up, work computer, personal computer, sleep, and repeat. A soul-sucking cycle of events. I've found things to break up this monotony like bowling or photography, however these things require more than bare minimum effort, such as requiring me to leave my house. So on days where I feel even the slightest bit lazy, computer time would easily beat those activities.

Luckily, a new hobby has entered my life to save the day, pool! By having the table in my house, it is so easy to just hop straight into a game, no matter what mood I'm in. This has been great for afternoons where I just needed to disconnect from technology and relax. If you don't have a low-effort no-technology passtime in your life, I highly recommend getting something going. However, pool has opened up a different can of worms for me.

I have a really hard time being a casually enjoyer of anything. My brain NEEDS to learn all the in's and out's of everything I am interested in and pool is no exception. I have been watching tons of content, learning what I need to do to improve my game and attempting to apply those skills. Pool being precision a game, these skills do not develop overnight, but I can feel myself getting better. I would like to track this progress, but sadly pool does not have a scoring system like other precision games like bowling.

Lucky for me, one of my favorite pool creators has a system to practice fundamentals and track how you're doing! This is called the "Runout Drill System" and I have created an entire page on my website to track my progress with it and remind myself of each step without accessing some external resource constantly :D. The steps ramp up very fast and the top steps are only achievable by the best players in the world, but the beginning steps still serve as an excellent way to track how my skills are improving. I would like to one day play in local pool tournaments (8 ball or 9 ball), so hopefully this will help me build confidence to sign up for one soon.
P.S. If anyone ever wants to come over and play pool, let me know!

title: A Miata and a Cayman Drive into a Bar

bodies.content: Finally, after waiting what felt like forever, the Porsche Cayman has hand controls installed and I can drive it! I believe it was very much in order for me to take PTO today to get them installed and drive it around to get accustomed to the new whip. Writing this blog, I just got home from my parents' house, using all the backroads, and wow!!! It tore that shit UP! The heavy steering of the Cayman gives you so much road feel and confidence in the corners that I've never felt in a car before. I guess that's why so many people praise the hydraulic steering and were sad to see it go in future Porsche generations.

That feeling of awe leads nicely into why I'm writing this blog, to throw my hat into the famous internet debate on Porsche Cayman vs Mazda Miata. Which is the best "daily car you can also bring to the track"? TL;DR, it is complicated!

I think when designing a car, there must be a balance of racecar considerations and commuter considerations and these two cars strike a slightly different balance. I think the Cayman has more racecar considerations (thus less commuter considerations) and the Miata has more commuter considerations (thus less racecar considerations).

For example, while driving the Cayman from the hand control shop to my parents' house in highway traffic, I could tell the Cayman did not want to be there. It handled the situation fine, but it was like a grouchy kid forced to go to the grocery store with mom and they are letting mom know they don't care to be there. The low RPM power is sluggish thanks to the tall gearing, the steering is heavy, and all the mechanical parts are loud. On the flip side, I've driven the Miata in tons of bumper-to-bumper traffic over the years and it was happy to do it. The Miata has plenty of low RPM power for zipping in-and-out of traffic, a lighter steering rack, and a quiet cabin (thank you hard top!).

However, while carving up backroads, I've never felt as confident in the Miata as I did today in the Cayman. Tearing up backroads at high undisclosed speeds, the Cayman was giving me so much road feedback and practically begging me to go faster. In the Miata, under the same conditions, the steering gets a little light and the car feels overall nervous.

Woah woah woah, hang on, pump the brakes on this blog. What are we doing? Am I saying the Miata is a commuter car not capable of high speed performance driving? No, but when it's pitted up against the Nordschleife-native Porsche Cayman on high speed driving. This can even be noticed in iRacing when both cars in their respective series go to my home track, VIR. The Miata class runs the tight and technical North course while the Cayman (GT4) class runs the high-speed sweeping grand prix course. They are built for different types of racing.

This little tangent really points out the absurdity of this internet debate. At the end of the day, both of these cars are impractical racecars. When I say the Miata is more practical or the Cayman is more of a racecar, the difference is marginal at best. The differences are all about tiny nuances that make these two cars who they are. To call one "the better option" as a blanket statement is insanity and you should not trust someone telling you that.

In my mind, the Cayman and the Miata are pieces of art in motion. You can tell the designers took zero shortcuts and made the car that they set out to make from the very beginning. No EPA eco-friendly B.S. or no executives dictating features usesless, they are unapologetically themselves and they fucking rule. I am so appreciative of both of these cars playing such a huge role in ensuring fun cars stay around for people to buy while also pushing motorsports forward. Obviously there are more models that do this, but the Cayman and the Miata are the two that have always stood out to me as the pillars of this idea.
So when people ask me, "Which would you buy, Porsche Cayman or Mazda Miata?", I am so grateful I get to say, "both"
